scope: P21 pre-implementation plan review for test/headless-cli-orchestrator.test.js below-500 split

blocking_findings.summary: The plan claimed 31 current root tests, but DeepSeek counted 33 test(...) calls. The mapping descriptions covered the tests, but stale numeric parity targets could allow a false 31/31 split artifact.
blocking_findings.required_change: Correct P21 to require 33 before / 33 after split parity based on actual current test names.

blocking_findings.summary: The proposed loop-projection shard packed nine tests into a budget that could exceed the 300-line near-threshold once imports are added.
blocking_findings.required_change: Split snapshot/loop persistence away from projected-action service-loop tests or explicitly justify a near-threshold shard.

blocking_findings.summary: The proposed child-provider shard packed six tests into a budget that could approach the 300-line near-threshold once imports are added.
blocking_findings.required_change: Move output-path parsing out of child-provider or otherwise reduce the shard budget pressure.

non_blocking_notes: DeepSeek confirmed the content-level domain mapping was otherwise sound and did not identify helper gate or circular dependency risks.
DeepSeek noted hardening and child-acceptance domains were structurally low-risk.

notes: P21 now uses 33 before / 33 after parity derived from actual test-name extraction.
Loop-projection budget risk is addressed by splitting loop persistence into its own shard.
Child-provider budget risk is addressed by moving output-path parsing into child acceptance.
Child-acceptance remains near the 300-line threshold and must be checked with actual line counts during implementation.
